instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, right? One minute it’s sunny, and the next, it’s pouring! Anyway, speaking of unpredictability, have you ever tried making diagrams? It’s like creating a little world of your own. So, I was thinking, wouldn’t it be cool to have a website where you can just dive into making these diagrams? You could create new ones, edit the ones you have, and just play around with different elements. I mean, who doesn’t love a good flowchart or an organizational chart? They’re like the backbone of any good project! And let’s not forget mind maps; those are just fantastic for brainstorming. Oh, and you could just drag and drop everything, which is super convenient. By the way, I had this amazing dinner last night, and it got me thinking about how important it is to have a clean layout. Maybe something like ghost white for the background would be nice, and slate gray for the details? Just a thought! But honestly, who cares about colors when you can just let your creativity flow?

Envision a backdrop that feels like a gentle morning mist and details that echo the calm of a quiet evening sky.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement an advanced to-do list web application where users can create, edit, and delete to-do items. The application should have functionalities for adding new tasks, marking tasks as completed, deleting tasks, filtering tasks, and searching tasks. Users should be able to browse all to-do items, create new tasks, edit existing tasks, and delete unnecessary tasks. Set all pages to have a lavender background and indigo components.

evaluation_checklist.question: Create a new task by filling out the "Add Task" form and submitting it.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The new task appears in the list of to-do items with the correct details and is visible under the user's account.

evaluation_checklist.question: Edit an existing task by changing its description and saving the changes.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The task's description is updated in the list and reflects the changes accurately.

evaluation_checklist.question: Mark a specific task as completed on the to-do list.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The task is visibly marked as completed, often indicated by a strikethrough, checkbox, or similar visual cue.

evaluation_checklist.question: Delete a task from the list by selecting the "Delete" option next to it.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The selected task is removed from the list and is no longer visible in the account.

evaluation_checklist.question: Use the search functionality to find a specific task by its title or keywords.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The search returns a relevant list of tasks that match the query, displaying intended search criteria clearly.

evaluation_checklist.question: Filter tasks to show only completed or uncompleted tasks.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The task list updates to display only tasks that match the selected filter criteria without affecting the unfiltered tasks' status.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify the site's navigation by clicking the provided menu items or links in the user interface.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Each navigation element correctly redirects the user to the intended page without errors, maintaining user session details.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it’s sunny and the next it’s pouring rain! Anyway, speaking of unpredictable things, have you ever tried to keep track of your tasks? It’s like trying to herd cats sometimes! So, I was thinking, wouldn’t it be great if we could have this advanced to-do list web application? You know, where users can create, edit, and delete to-do items? I mean, imagine being able to add new tasks, and then there’s that satisfying moment when you mark them as completed. Oh, and deleting tasks can be so therapeutic, especially when you realize you’ve been holding onto things that just don’t matter anymore, like that awful dinner I had last week! And filtering tasks? That’s like sorting through all the gossip you hear at work, trying to find the juicy bits. Plus, searching tasks is a must! And I think all the pages should have this lovely lavender background, you know, something calming, with indigo components to really make it pop. It’s just like how I wish my garden would look if I could ever get around to tending to it!

instruction: Please implement an advanced to-do list web application where users can create, edit, and delete to-do items. The application should have functionalities for adding new tasks, marking tasks as completed, deleting tasks, filtering tasks, and searching tasks. Users should be able to browse all to-do items, create new tasks, edit existing tasks, and delete unnecessary tasks. ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a detective agency case management app for managing cases and tracking work hours. The app should have functionalities for real-time reporting, data collection and storage, work hours tracking, estimated expense sheet generation, and service assignment sheet. Users should be able to browse case information, submit real-time reports, log work hours, and view estimated expense sheets and service assignment sheets. Apply beige as the background; style all components with saddle brown.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that case information is displayed accurately and can be browsed.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user can navigate through different cases, and each case displays detailed and correct information as per database or test data.

evaluation_checklist.question: Attempt to submit a real-time report on a case.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The report is successfully submitted, and a confirmation message is displayed to the user. The report appears in the case's report history.

evaluation_checklist.question: Log work hours for a specific case.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Work hours are successfully logged, and the total hours are updated in real-time in the case details.

evaluation_checklist.question: Generate an estimated expense sheet for a particular case.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: An estimated expense sheet is generated accurately, reflecting the required calculations and displaying all necessary details to the user.

evaluation_checklist.question: Access the service assignment sheet and ensure it is displayed correctly.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The service assignment sheet is accessible and presents all relevant information clearly associated with the selected case.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the navigation functionality from the case management screen back to the dashboard.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user can successfully navigate back to the main dashboard without any errors.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'beige' as the background color and 'saddle brown' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'beige' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'saddle brown', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, right? Like, one minute it's sunny, and the next, it's pouring rain. Anyway, speaking of unpredictable things, have you ever noticed how detective agencies always seem to have the most interesting cases? I mean, if I were to implement a detective agency case management app, it would totally need to manage cases and track work hours effectively. And you know, with all the gossip going around about competitors, it’s crucial to have real-time reporting and data collection features. Oh, and by the way, did I tell you about that amazing dinner I had last night? It was so good! Anyway, back to the app – it should definitely include work hours tracking and maybe even an expense sheet for all those late-night stakeouts. Users should be able to browse case information and submit reports, which is super important, especially when you’re juggling so many things at once, like what to cook for dinner or how to deal with the neighbor's loud music. And don’t forget, we need a service assignment sheet too. Oh, and I think beige would be a nice background color, but saddle brown for the components would really tie everything together, don’t you think?

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a detective agency case management app for managing cases and tracking work hours. The app should have functionalities for real-time reporting, data collection and storage, work hours tracking, estimated expense sheet generation, and service assignment sheet. Users should be able to browse case information, submit real-time reports, log work hours, and view estimated expense sheets and service assignment sheets. Apply beige as the background; style all components with saddle brown.

instruction: Please implement a detective agency case management app that has functionalities for managing cases and tracking work hours, but ensure that all text is invisible and all buttons are unclickable. The app should also have a bright neon green background while styling all components with deep black.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a risk assessment website for conducting risk assessments and generating reports. The website should have functionalities for selecting risk assessment items, auto-populating data, generating charts, and creating reports. Users should be able to select risk assessment items, and the website will automatically fill in relevant data from a background database, generate charts, and create reports. The website should also have a subscription feature, allowing users to access the risk assessment tool. Define the background as alice blue and components as steel blue.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test risk assessment item selection.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to select risk assessment items from a list, and selected items should be clearly highlighted or checked.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify auto-population of data.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: When a user selects a risk assessment item, the relevant data should automatically populate from the background database without errors or delays.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test chart generation.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: After selecting risk assessment items, the website should generate accurate charts reflecting the selected data, which should be visible and correctly labeled.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test report creation functionality.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to generate a downloadable report that accurately includes the selected risk assessment items, populated data, and generated charts.

evaluation_checklist.question: Check the subscription feature.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to successfully subscribe, ensuring their access to the risk assessment tool is granted immediately upon subscription.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test navigation between pages.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to seamlessly navigate through various pages such as login, dashboard, and reporting without encountering broken links or errors.

evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'alice blue' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'steel blue', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it's sunny, and the next it's pouring rain. Speaking of unpredictability, I really need to implement a risk assessment website for conducting those risk assessments and generating reports, you know? It’s like when you’re trying to pick what to have for dinner, and you just can’t decide. Anyway, the website should have functionalities for selecting those risk assessment items, and I hope it auto-populates data like how I wish my plants would grow without me having to water them every day! Oh, and generating charts is essential, just like how I chart my daily coffee intake. I mean, who doesn’t love a good chart? And creating reports is a must too, especially when you have to explain to your friends why you’re still using that old laptop. Users should totally be able to select risk assessment items, and it would be super convenient if the website automatically fills in relevant data from a background database, much like how I wish my fridge would magically refill itself. Also, I heard that the subscription feature is important, kind of like how I subscribe to all those streaming services but never have time to watch anything. Oh, and let's not forget to define the background as alice blue, which is a lovely color, and components as steel blue, just like the color of my neighbor's car that I keep complaining about!

instruction: Imagine crafting a sanctuary where individuals can navigate the waters of uncertainty, exploring the depths of potential risks while creating a tapestry of insights. This digital haven should invite users to handpick the elements of their exploration, seamlessly weaving in the threads of information from a hidden treasure trove of knowledge. As they journey through this experience, vibrant visuals will blossom, illuminating their findings like stars in a night sky, and they will be able to compose their own chronicles of discovery. ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a risk assessment website for conducting risk assessments and generating reports. The website should have functionalities for selecting risk assessment items, auto-populating data, generating charts, and creating reports. Users should be able to select risk assessment items, and the website will automatically fill in relevant data from a background database, generate charts, and create reports. The website should also have a subscription feature, allowing users to access the risk assessment tool. Define the background as alice blue and components as steel blue.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test risk assessment item selection.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test chart generation.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: After selecting risk assessment items, the website should generate accurate charts reflecting the selected data, which should be visible and correctly labeled.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test report creation functionality.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to generate a downloadable report that accurately includes the selected risk assessment items, populated data, and generated charts.

evaluation_checklist.question: Check the subscription feature.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to successfully subscribe, ensuring their access to the risk assessment tool is granted immediately upon subscription.

instruction: Please implement a risk assessment website for conducting risk assessments and generating reports that has functionalities for selecting risk assessment items, auto-populating data, generating charts, and creating reports. ground_truth_instruction: Please implement an entertainment directory website to display band information and advertisements. The website should have band registration and login functionality, allowing band members to input their bio, provide picture links and audio sample links, and provide direct links to YouTube videos. Additionally, the website should allow advertisers to register, log in, and upload their ad content. Users should be able to browse band information, listen to audio samples, watch videos, and view advertisements. Implement floral white for the page background and dark goldenrod for the elements.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify band registration form functionality.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The form successfully captures all required fields, including band name, bio, and contact information. Submission leads to a confirmation message without any errors.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test band login functionality.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Using valid credentials allows band members access to their dashboard without error messages. Invalid credentials provide an appropriate error message.

evaluation_checklist.question: Check the ability for band members to provide a profile picture link.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The picture upload functionality accepts image files and displays the uploaded image in the band's profile without errors or distortions.

evaluation_checklist.question: Validate the upload of audio sample links by band members.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Audio files are uploaded successfully, the files can be played from the band's profile page, and playback is smooth without errors.

evaluation_checklist.question: Confirm functionality for band members to add YouTube video links.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Added YouTube links appear correctly in the band's profile, and videos play within the website without navigating away.

evaluation_checklist.question: Assess the registration form functionality for advertisers.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The form captures all the necessary information without errors, and submission results in a success confirmation message.

evaluation_checklist.question: Evaluate login functionality for advertisers.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Using valid credentials allows advertisers to log in and access their dashboard successfully. Invalid credentials result in an appropriate error message.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'floral white' as the background color and 'dark goldenrod' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'floral white' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'dark goldenrod', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it's sunny and the next it's pouring rain! Speaking of unpredictable, have you heard about that new band that's been making waves? Anyway, back to the task at hand – it’s really important to create this entertainment directory website, you know? Like, imagine a place where bands can totally register and log in, sharing their bios, which is so essential for their fans! And, oh, don’t forget about those picture links and audio samples – they really need to showcase their talent, right? Plus, it's super cool if they can link to their YouTube videos. I mean, I was just watching some old concert footage last night, and it was amazing! Oh, and advertisers should totally be able to register and log in too, so they can upload their content. It’s like having a community where users can browse all this band info, listen to audio samples, watch videos, and see those ads pop up. By the way, I had the most delicious dinner yesterday, it was pasta with a creamy sauce, and it reminded me of how important it is to have a nice background for the website – maybe floral white for the page and dark goldenrod for the elements? Just a thought!

instruction: Please implement an entertainment directory website to display band information and advertisements. The website should have band registration and login functionality, allowing band members to input their bio, provide picture links and audio sample links, and provide direct links to YouTube videos. Additionally, the website should allow advertisers to register, log in, and upload their ad content. Users should be able to browse band information, listen to audio samples, watch videos, and view advertisements. Implement a bright red background for the page and a dark goldenrod for the elements, while also ensuring that the text is completely invisible.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a startup article publishing mini-program to display entrepreneurial experiences and stories. The mini-program should have functions for browsing articles, searching, collecting, and sharing. Users should be able to browse articles in different categories, search for articles of interest, collect favorite articles, and share articles on other platforms. The mini-program should also have user registration and login functions, allowing users to manage their collections and reading history. Style the layout with old lace in the background and rosy brown for the interface.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify Article Browsing
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users can successfully browse articles under different categories. Selecting a category should display a list of relevant articles without errors or missing content.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test Search Functionality
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: When a user enters a query into the search bar and submits it, the system should display a list of articles that match the search criteria, reflecting accurate and relevant results based on the query.

evaluation_checklist.question: Check Article Collection Feature
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to click a "collect" button on an article, which will then be added to their collection. The collected articles should be accessible from a dedicated section, accurately displaying all saved articles.

evaluation_checklist.question: Validate Article Sharing Capability
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to share articles to other platforms via a functional share button. Upon clicking, options for sharing (e.g., social media, email) should be displayed, and the selected method should successfully transmit the article link.

evaluation_checklist.question: Assess User Registration Process
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: New users should be able to register by providing necessary information (e.g., username, email, password). The registration process should complete without errors, and users should receive confirmation of successful registration.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test User Login Functionality
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Existing users should be able to log in using their credentials. Upon successful login, users should be directed to their personalized homepage, where they can access their collections and reading history.

evaluation_checklist.question: Validate Reading History Tracking
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: As users read articles, these articles should appear in their reading history section. The history should update automatically and display a correct and chronological list of previously read articles.

evaluation_checklist.question: Examine Navigation to Different Sections
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to navigate smoothly between sections (e.g., categories, search, collections, profile) via a navigation menu. The transition between sections should be seamless, with minimal loading time and no broken links.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it’s sunny and the next it’s pouring rain. Anyway, speaking of unpredictability, have you ever tried to implement a startup article publishing mini-program? It’s really fascinating how people share their entrepreneurial experiences and stories. I mean, there are so many articles out there! You could have functions for browsing articles—like, imagine scrolling through different categories while sipping your morning coffee. And then, of course, searching for articles of interest is crucial, right? Oh, and don’t even get me started on collecting favorite articles! It’s like keeping a scrapbook of your thoughts, but digital. Sharing articles on other platforms is also a must; you know, like how I shared that funny meme with my friend last week. And user registration is so important too—like, I had to register for that new app just to keep track of my reading history. By the way, did I mention I had the most delicious dinner last night? Anyway, the layout should definitely have old lace in the background and rosy brown for the interface, just to give it that warm, inviting feel.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a social website for user registration, making friends, and sharing. The website should have a registration function, allowing users to create an account and log in. Users should be able to invite friends to join the website and vote on content. instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it's sunny and the next it's pouring rain, which reminds me of how people just love to complain about their neighbors, right? Anyway, speaking of neighbors, can you imagine implementing a social website where people can register, make friends, and share all those little details about their lives? Like, I mean, it should totally have a registration function, allowing users to create an account and log in, but honestly, who even has time for that when you’re trying to figure out what to have for dinner? Oh, and don't forget, users should be able to invite friends to join the website and vote on content, like that time I tried to get my friends to vote on the best pizza place in town. And pictures! Everyone loves uploading pictures of their pets or food, right? They should earn points for that too! By the way, did I tell you about my cat? He’s been acting so weird lately. Anyway, the website should definitely have a personal homepage that displays user information and updates, and I think light cyan would be a nice touch for the background, with cadet blue for the components. But honestly, who cares about colors when there are so many interesting things happening around us?

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it’s sunny and the next it’s pouring rain, and speaking of rain, I really need to book some tickets for my trip next month. Anyway, I think it would be great if there was this platform, you know, where you could easily search for bus and railway tickets, just like how I search for the best coffee shops around town. And wouldn’t it be nice if it could show you real-time seat availability? I mean, I once missed a train because I didn’t check, and that was such a hassle! Oh, and secure payments are a must—like, I can’t believe how many scams are out there. I just want to get my booking confirmations via email or SMS without any fuss. Also, user account management features are super important, like being able to log in and see my booking history. Sometimes I forget where I’ve been, you know? And ticket printing options would be handy, especially if I’m in a rush. By the way, did I mention how cozy it would be to have a responsive design? It should look good on mobile and desktop, just like my favorite sweater that fits perfectly. Oh, and let’s not forget about those snowy backgrounds and dim gray components—so aesthetically pleasing!

instruction:
Create a serene travel experience that allows users to effortlessly explore and secure their journeys on buses and trains. Imagine a space where they can easily search for tickets, witness the ebb and flow of available seats and prices, and make secure payments that feel as safe as a warm embrace. Once their journeys are booked, they should receive confirmations that flutter into their inbox or resonate through their phones like a gentle reminder. 

Envision a welcoming environment where users can manage their travel identities, reflecting their past adventures and offering them the chance to hold tangible memories in their hands. The design should flow gracefully, adapting seamlessly to the contours of both small and grand devices, creating an atmosphere of comfort and ease. Picture all backgrounds as soft blankets of snow, while the components whisper in gentle shades of twilight gray, evoking a sense of calm and tranquility.

instruction: Please implement a minimalistic bus and railway ticket booking platform where users can search and book bus and railway tickets, view real-time seat availability and prices, make secure payments, and receive booking confirmations via email or SMS. ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a golf travel package booking website that sells golf travel packages. The website should have functionalities for customer request handling, booking confirmation, online quotes, customer relationship management, and supplier management. Users can initiate requests through the website homepage form submission, and the system will collect information based on customer preferences and dynamically apply hotel, flight, transport, and golf conditions. Customers can confirm bookings online, and the system will generate digital vouchers and invoices. Assign whitesmoke to body backgrounds and dark cyan to main components.

evaluation_checklist.question: Check the functionality of the initial request form submission on the homepage.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The form should successfully accept user inputs such as travel dates, destination, and golfing preferences and trigger a confirmation message or prompt upon submission.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify the ability to book a travel package through the website.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to navigate through the booking process, select a travel package, and receive a clear confirmation message on successful booking.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the online quote generation feature for a selected travel package.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website should dynamically generate an accurate and detailed quote based on user preferences, including hotel, flight, transport, and golf conditions.

evaluation_checklist.question: Validate the content and accuracy of the digital voucher generated after booking confirmation.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The digital voucher should include specific travel package details, booking confirmation number, and customer information accurately corresponding to the mock booking data submitted.

evaluation_checklist.question: Evaluate the responsiveness of the website's navigation menu.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The navigation menu should function smoothly and resize or adapt appropriately across different devices (desktop, tablet, smartphone) without layout issues or hidden options.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the supplier management interface for adding or updating a golf package supplier.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user should be able to successfully add or update supplier details with confirmation that changes have been saved in the system.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the customer relationship management (CRM) feature by updating customer preferences.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Customer information should be correctly updated within the CRM, and any changes should reflect immediately where relevant (e.g., personalized suggestions or quotes).

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it’s sunny and the next it’s pouring! Anyway, have you ever tried to book a golf travel package? It’s quite the task, really. So, imagine a website where you can actually handle customer requests, like, you know, when someone wants to know about booking confirmations or maybe even get online quotes. It’s like when you’re trying to figure out if you should go to that new restaurant or stick with your favorite diner. Speaking of which, I had this amazing dinner last night, and it made me think about how we should have a great customer relationship management system in place, just like how I maintain my friendships! Oh, and don’t forget about supplier management, which is super important too. Users could just fill out a form on the homepage, and the system would gather all this info based on what they like, kind of like how I choose my outfits based on my mood. And then, they could confirm their bookings online, and voila, digital vouchers and invoices would pop up like magic! By the way, I think whitesmoke would be a nice touch for body backgrounds, and dark cyan for the main components would really make it stand out, don’t you think?

instruction: Create a serene oasis for golf enthusiasts, where the journey to their dream getaway unfolds effortlessly. ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a travel reimbursement and payment system for employee travel expenses. The system should have functionalities for booking tickets, hotels, and trains, generating settlements, submitting reimbursement applications, and more. Users should be able to book tickets, hotels, and trains, generate settlements report, submit reimbursement applications, and view reimbursement status. Use white for container backgrounds and navy for component visuals.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it’s sunny and the next it’s pouring rain! Anyway, speaking of travel, I really think we should implement a travel reimbursement and payment system for employee travel expenses. It’s such a hassle when you have to figure out how to book tickets, hotels, and trains, right? I mean, who even has the time? And don’t get me started on generating settlements; it’s like a maze! Plus, submitting reimbursement applications can be such a chore. Oh, and did you hear about that new restaurant that opened up downtown? They say the food is amazing! But back to the point, we really need to make sure users can book tickets, hotels, and trains easily, generate settlements reports, and check on their reimbursement status. By the way, I had the most delicious dinner last night; it was so comforting! Anyway, let’s keep the container backgrounds white and the visuals navy, just to keep things looking sharp amidst all this chaos!

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a travel reimbursement and payment system for employee travel expenses. The system should have functionalities for booking tickets, hotels, and trains, generating settlements, submitting reimbursement applications, and more. Users should be able to book tickets, hotels, and trains, generate settlements report, submit reimbursement applications, and view reimbursement status. Use white for container backgrounds and navy for component visuals.

instruction: Create a journey reimbursement and payment experience that feels like a seamless adventure for employees. This system should allow users to embark on their travel quests, finding their way to cozy havens and smooth rides, while also ensuring that their journeys are accounted for with ease. Users should have the freedom to explore their travel options, receive a clear map of their financial settlements, share their reimbursement tales, and keep an eye on their reimbursement journeys. Let the backdrop of this experience be as pure as fresh snow, while the components reflect the depth of a midnight sea.
